worthClicking.author: @gdb
worthClicking.why: Greg Brockman's thread-opening post frames OpenAI's full launch week as a coherent platform shift — agents that work, remember, and run independently — worth reading as a strategic positioning statement.

worthClicking.author: @gdb
worthClicking.why: GPT-5.5 topping CursorBench is the most concrete competitive signal against Claude Code this week — a benchmark that matters for coding-tool buyers.

worthClicking.author: @ClaudeDevs
worthClicking.why: Claude Code's web/mobile refresh ships teleport (web↔CLI handoff) and a redesigned UI — directly relevant for evaluating Claude Code as a product.

worthClicking.author: @levie
worthClicking.why: Levie's Jevons paradox argument — AI productivity gains increase demand for labor rather than shrinking it — is the clearest counter-narrative to the AI-replaces-jobs thesis and matters for enterprise sales framing.

worthClicking.author: @aakashgupta
worthClicking.why: Concrete 4-component agent architecture framework (intelligence, memory, tools, orchestration) is the most actionable AI product strategy post in this batch.

worthClicking.author: @aakashgupta
worthClicking.why: The claim that AI PM interviews have shifted from product cases to system design is a direct signal about what the market values in AI product leadership right now.

worthClicking.author: @aakashgupta
worthClicking.why: A Claude Routine that auto-preps meeting briefs from Calendar + Gmail is a sharp, concrete example of agentic automation an operator could ship or demo today.

worthClicking.author: @RoundtableSpace
worthClicking.why: A CLAUDE.md file that implements Karpathy's LLM coding critique as four enforceable rules is a practical fix operators using Claude Code can apply immediately.

worthClicking.author: @emollick
worthClicking.why: Mollick flags that multi-agent org design and benchmarking agents-in-concert is the next unsolved frontier — relevant for anyone building or selling agent workflows.

voices.why: Greg Brockman is narrating OpenAI's full platform strategy this week in real time — high signal for competitive AI product intelligence.

Consistent output on agent architecture and AI PM role evolution, with concrete frameworks rather than takes — relevant to two of the operator's three interests, though heavy on newsletter self-promo.
Rare voice applying classical economic frameworks (Jevons) to AI labor displacement — useful for enterprise sales narratives.
Flags multi-agent org design as the next unsolved problem with genuine intellectual weight behind it.

examples: @aakashgupta newsletter self-promo spam (8+ identical 'subscribe to my newsletter' posts with near-zero engagement)
@RoundtableSpace all-caps clickbait formatting on crypto/markets/farming tangents with no original analysis
lifestyle/vibe posts (travel takes, dopamine science, imposter syndrome, craft obsession quotes)

fading.theme: model competition (Opus 4.7 vs GPT-5.5, Mythos holdback)
